meta.description: Governance-as-Code ruleset and AI vendor fingerprint database for the External Transparency Agent.

legal_basis.authoritative_statute: Texas HB 149 (Responsible Artificial Intelligence Governance Act / TRAIGA)
legal_basis.codification: Tex. Bus. & Com. Code, Chapter 552 (Artificial Intelligence Protection)
legal_basis.alias_note: 'SB 149' is a known label slip retained ONLY for legacy-document resolution. HB 149 / Ch. 552 is authoritative.
legal_basis.disclaimer: Engineering artifact, not legal advice. Findings are candidate signals for human/legal review.

AI_Vendor_Fingerprints.module_description: Weighted signatures for the fingerprint engine. ALL signatures are verification_status='unverified_candidate' unless noted.

vendors.note: Widget is typically GTM-injected or async-loaded. Static-fallback detection relies on text_marker_regex against raw HTML and GTM container inspection in crawler.

indicators.note: Matches citibot.io or citibot.net anywhere in rendered HTML. Lewisville confirmed uses citibot.net.
Matches webchat-ui.citibot.net — confirmed embed subdomain for Lewisville deployment.
GTM container tag label patterns surfaced by crawler GTM container inspection.

vendors.note: AI civic Q&A widget observed live on cityofdenton.com 2026-07-05: iframe id='repd-embed' src=embed.repd.us/denton, 'Ask City Hall' launcher bubble.
Vendor named in project brief; existence and signatures must be confirmed before use.
Confirmed on cityofdenton.com 2026-06-27 via Chrome MCP deep scan. Requires TRAIGA 552.051 disclosure.

Observed live on www.gptx.org (Grand Prairie) 2026-07-07: <elevenlabs-convai agent-id='agent_...'> custom element rendered in shadow DOM (no iframe), loader script https://unpkg.com/@elevenlabs/convai-widget-embed, runtime calls api.us.elevenlabs.io. Deployed by integrator WhitegloveAI (city announcement 2026-05-04). Opening widget shows T&C consent naming 'this AI agent'. Detection relies on host element tag + loader URL in rendered HTML; script host is generic unpkg.com so do NOT fingerprint unpkg.

indicators.note: Custom element tag in rendered HTML. Fires alone (>= 0.6 threshold).
unpkg loader script URL fragment in HTML.

vendors.note: Catch-all heuristic for unbranded conversational widgets; low individual weights to limit false positives.

Internal_Governance_Module.module_description: RESERVED for future inline use against City-operated AI systems. Not evaluated by the external observer.

rules.title: AI inventory and governance ownership documented
rules.remediation: Maintain an AI system inventory with assigned governance owner.

rules.title: Internal/adversarial testing program evidence
rules.citation: Tex. Bus. & Com. Code Ch. 552 (safe-harbor provisions)
rules.remediation: Document red-team / adversarial testing to support good-faith defense.

External_Transparency_Module.module_description: Evaluated by the External Transparency Agent against publicly observable evidence only.

rules.title: AI-interaction disclosure present
rules.pass_condition: A statement that the user is interacting with an AI system is present in or adjacent to the asset.
rules.remediation: Add a clear statement that the user is interacting with an AI system.

rules.title: Disclosure shown before or at time of interaction
rules.pass_condition: Disclosure is visible before/at first interaction, not buried in footer-only links.
rules.remediation: Surface the disclosure at the entry point of the AI asset.

rules.title: Disclosure is clear, conspicuous, plain-language, no dark patterns
rules.pass_condition: Disclosure language is plain, prominent, and not obscured by dark-pattern design.
rules.remediation: Rewrite disclosure in plain language and increase prominence.

rules.title: Privacy policy linked, reachable, and addresses automated processing
rules.citation: Tex. Bus. & Com. Code Ch. 552 (transparency objectives)
rules.pass_condition: A reachable privacy policy is linked from the asset context and references automated/AI processing.
rules.remediation: Link a reachable privacy policy that addresses AI/automated processing.

rules.title: Biometric capture notice/consent visible when biometric indicators present
rules.citation: Tex. Bus. & Com. Code Ch. 552 (biometric provisions)
rules.pass_condition: Where biometric capture is indicated, notice/consent is surfaced to the user.
rules.remediation: Surface biometric notice/consent; confirm lawful basis with counsel.

violation_template.description: Shape of every violation record. cure_period_status and days_remaining are REQUIRED per project spec.

cure_period_status.description: true while inside the active 60-day cure window.

evidence.description: Indicators that fired; URLs; no end-user PII.

Safe_Harbor_Module.module_description: Municipal AI Profile v1.0 — a right-sized profile of the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0 core functions + Generative AI Profile, NIST AI 600-1) for Texas municipalities. Powers the TRAIGA safe-harbor readiness checklist and the Alignment Statement generator.

legal_basis.citation: Tex. Bus. & Com. Code Sec. 552.105(c)-(e) (HB 149 enrolled)
legal_basis.mechanism: 552.105(c): rebuttable presumption of reasonable care. 552.105(e): no liability where the defendant discovered the violation through (B) testing, including adversarial testing, or (D) an internal review process substantially complying with the most recent NIST 'Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework: Generative Artificial Intelligence Profile' or another nationally or internationally recognized AI risk management framework.
legal_basis.product_theory: The external scanner IS 552.105(e)(B) testing; the profile checklist + scan cadence + cure loop IS the (e)(D) internal review process. Evidence is machine-verified where possible, attested otherwise.
legal_basis.disclaimer: Engineering artifact, not legal advice. Alignment Statements require counsel review before use.

controls.title: Accountable AI official designated
controls.plain: One named person owns AI governance for the city.
controls.attest_hint: Name the role (e.g., CIO) in the notes; update when the role changes.

controls.title: AI use policy adopted
controls.plain: The city has a written AI use policy in force.
controls.attest_hint: Generate a draft with the AI Use Policy button, then attest adoption with date/authority.

controls.title: Staff acceptable-use rules communicated
controls.plain: Employees know what AI tools they may and may not use.
controls.attest_hint: Reference the training/communication date and channel.

controls.title: Governance decisions leave an audit trail
controls.plain: Actions in the registry are logged: who did what, when.

controls.title: AI inventory exists and is current
controls.plain: The city keeps a live list of its AI systems.

controls.title: Each AI system has documented purpose and owner
controls.plain: Every AI system on the list says what it's for and who owns it.

controls.title: Public-facing AI is actively discovered
controls.plain: The city's website is scanned for AI it may not know it has, including vendor-embedded tools.

controls.title: Disclosure compliance is tested against HB 149
controls.plain: Every public AI is checked for the state-required 'you are talking to an AI' notice.
controls.nist_ref: GAI Profile MS-2.5 / AI RMF MEASURE 2.5

controls.title: Monitoring runs on a recurring cadence
controls.plain: Checks happen automatically on a schedule, not once a year.

controls.title: Employee AI usage has visibility
controls.plain: The city can see when staff use AI tools with city data (Sentinel), or has a stated interim process.
controls.attest_hint: If Sentinel is not yet deployed, attest the interim process (e.g., policy + spot checks).

controls.title: Findings are tracked with statutory cure deadlines
controls.plain: Every problem found gets a 60-day legal clock and is tracked to closure.

controls.title: Remediation completes within the cure window
controls.plain: Problems get fixed before the legal deadline expires.

controls.title: AI incident and complaint intake exists
controls.plain: Residents and staff have a way to report AI problems, and reports get handled.
controls.attest_hint: Reference the intake channel (311, web form, email) and the owning department.

controls.title: Vendor AI is reviewed before and during use
controls.plain: AI bought from vendors gets a look-over: what data it touches and what the contract says.
controls.attest_hint: Reference the procurement review step or contract clause used.
